expectations.rubric: Tests assert behavior (input/output), not implementation details. At least one edge case (negative numbers or zero) is tested.

The tester agent's test suite includes boundary condition tests (zero, negative numbers, large numbers) and at least one assertion designed to catch a hardcoded return value. Tests assert on specific expected values, not truthiness checks like toBeDefined(). Score 0.8+ if boundary tests and anti-hardcode test present, 0.5-0.7 if only basic assertions, below 0.5 if tests are weak.
expectations.description: Subagent quality: tester adversarial test design

expectations.rubric: The executor agent's implementation is minimal — it solves the spec requirements without unnecessary abstractions, unused parameters, or speculative features. Error handling matches what the spec requires, nothing more. The code follows the project's TypeScript conventions. Score 0.8+ if implementation is minimal and correct, 0.5-0.7 if correct but includes unnecessary code, below 0.5 if incorrect or significantly over-engineered.

expectations.description: No design produced for vague request

expectations.rubric: The skill asked clarifying questions or refused to proceed with the vague request 'Make it better'. It did NOT hallucinate a design or produce artifacts without understanding the requirement.

The semantic report identifies at least one of: empty catch block (fail-open handling), stack trace in error response (error data leakage), or unchecked return value. Findings are WARN severity, not BLOCK.

expectations.description: Build should NOT produce implementation files from a spec with no ACs

expectations.rubric: The build output indicates that the spec is deficient — missing acceptance criteria, unable to proceed, or similar. The build should NOT silently produce implementation code. Score 0.8+ if explicitly mentions missing/absent acceptance criteria. Score 0.5-0.7 if mentions spec is incomplete. Below 0.5 if silently proceeds.

prompt_args.error_output:
Test failure: processor.test.ts
  FAIL: processes all elements
    Expected: [2, 4, 6, 8, 10]
    Received: [2, 4, 6, 8]
    Last element (5) was not processed.

expectations.description: Off-by-one bug is fixed (loop includes last element)

expectations.rubric: The diagnosis identifies the off-by-one error in processor.ts — specifically that the loop condition `i < numbers.length - 1` skips the last element. The fix changes the loop to include all elements. Score 0.8+ if root cause identified at correct file/line with correct fix. Score 0.5-0.7 if root cause identified but fix is indirect. Below 0.5 if root cause is missed.
expectations.description: Diagnosis quality: identifies off-by-one root cause

expectations.rubric: The AUDIT.md identifies at least 2 of these 3 planted issues: (1) circular dependency between moduleA.ts and moduleB.ts, (2) unused exported function unusedHelper in utils.ts, (3) inconsistent file naming — order_handler.ts uses snake_case violating the constitution's camelCase rule. Each finding should have a severity classification. Score 0.8+ if 2+ found with severity, 0.5-0.7 if 1 found, below 0.5 if none found.
expectations.description: Audit finding accuracy: identifies planted architectural issues

expectations.rubric: The tester agent wrote tests that include: (1) boundary conditions — consuming exactly capacity tokens, consuming 0 available tokens, refill to exactly capacity (never exceeds). (2) Error path tests — negative capacity config, zero tokens consume attempt. (3) At least one test designed to catch a hardcoded implementation — e.g., testing multiple different consume amounts or verifying token count changes after consume. Score 0.8+ if all 3 present, 0.5-0.7 if 2 of 3, below 0.5 if only happy path.
expectations.description: Subagent quality: tester adversarial test coverage

expectations.rubric: The executor agent's rate limiter implementation: (1) uses no external dependencies beyond the TypeScript standard library, (2) correctly handles the refill-over-time logic without race conditions in the calculation, (3) does not include features beyond what the spec requires (no HTTP middleware, no Redis integration, no async patterns unless spec demands them). Score 0.8+ if minimal and correct, 0.5-0.7 if correct but over-engineered, below 0.5 if incorrect or missing spec requirements.

expectations.description: sw-design emits the three-line gate handoff format with no four-section template leakage
sw-plan emits the three-line gate handoff format
sw-verify emits the three-line gate handoff format
sw-ship emits the three-line gate handoff format
